step_by_step_thinking:
The patient experienced pain in the socket on the third day without fever or swelling, indicative of alveolar osteitis (dry socket). According to the articles, irrigation of the socket with saline or chlorhexidine, combined with placement of a medicated dressing such as zinc oxide eugenol, is the preferred initial management.
\boxed{A} Based on the presented information about managing alveolar osteitis/dry sockets, the best course of action here appears to involve irrigation of the socket and application of a soothing dressing.
